text: Ṭā, Seen, Meem.
tl: Taa-Seeen-Meeem

text: These are verses of the clear Book.
tl: Tilka Aayaatul Kitaabil mubeen

text: We recite to you from the news of Moses and Pharaoh in truth for a people who believe.
tl: Natloo 'alaika min naba-i Moosaa Wa-Fir'awna bilhaqqi liqawminy yu'miinoon

text: Indeed, Pharaoh exalted himself in the land and made its people into factions, oppressing a sector among them, slaughtering their [newborn] sons and keeping their females alive. Indeed, he was of the corrupters.
tl: Inna Fir'awna 'alaa fil ardi Wa-ja'ala ahlahaa shiya'ai yastad'ifu taaa'ifatam minhum yuzabbihu abnaaa'ahum Wa-yastahyee nisaaa'ahum; innahoo kaana minal mufsideen

text: And We wanted to confer favor upon those who were oppressed in the land and make them leaders and make them inheritors
tl: Wa-nureedu an namunna 'alal lazeenas tud'ifoo fil ardi Wa-naj'alahum a'immatanw Wa-naj'alahumul waariseen

text: And establish them in the land and show Pharaoh and [his minister] Hāmān and their soldiers through them that which they had feared.
tl: Wa-numakkina lahum fil ardi Wa-nuriya Fir'awna Wa-Haamaana Wa-junoodahumaa minhum maa kaanoo yahzaroon

text: And We inspired to the mother of Moses, \"Suckle him; but when you fear for him, cast him into the river and do not fear and do not grieve. Indeed, We will return him to you and will make him [one] of the messengers.\"
tl: Wa-awhainaaa ilaaa ummi Moosaaa an ardi'eehi faizaa khifti 'alaihi fa-alqeehi fil yammi Wa-laa takhaafee Wa-laa tahzaneee innaa raaaddoohu ilaiki Wa-jaa'iloohu minal mursaleen

text: And the family of Pharaoh picked him up [out of the river] so that he would become to them an enemy and a [cause of] grief. Indeed, Pharaoh and Hāmān and their soldiers were deliberate sinners.
tl: Faltaqatahooo Aalu Fir'awna li-yakoona lahum 'aduwwanw Wa-hazanaa; inna Fir'awna Wa-Haamaana Wa-junooda-humaa kaanoo khaati'een

text: And the wife of Pharaoh said, \"[He will be] a comfort of the eye [i.e., pleasure] for me and for you. Do not kill him; perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son.\" And they perceived not.
tl: Wa-qaalatim ra-atu Fir'awna qurratu 'aynil lee Wa-lak; laa taqtuloohu 'asaaa aien yanfa'anaa aw nattakhizahoo waladanw Wa-hum laa yash'uroon

text: And the heart of Moses' mother became empty [of all else]. She was about to disclose [the matter concerning] him had We not bound fast her heart that she would be of the believers.
tl: Wa-asbaha fu'aadu ummi Moosaa faarighan in kaadat latubdee bihee law laaa arrabatnaa 'alaa qalbihaa litakoona minal mu'mineen

text: And she said to his sister, \"Follow him\"; so she watched him from a distance while they perceived not.
tl: Wa-qaalat li-ukhtihee qusseehi fabasurat bihee 'an junubinw wahum laa yash'uroon

text: And We had prevented from him [all] wet nurses before, so she said, \"Shall I direct you to a household that will be responsible for him for you while they are to him [for his upbringing] sincere?\"
tl: Wa-harramnaa 'alaihil maraadi'a min qablu faqaalat hal adullukum 'alaaa ahli baitiny yakfuloonahoo lakum Wa-hum lahoo naasihoon

text: So We restored him to his mother that she might be content and not grieve and that she would know that the promise of Allāh is true. But most of them [i.e., the people] do not know.
tl: Faradadnaahu ilaa ummihee kai taqarra 'ainuhaa Wa-laa tahzana Wa-lita'lama anna wa'dal laahi haqqunw Wa-laakinna aksarahum laa ya'lamoon

text: And when he attained his full strength and was [mentally] mature, We bestowed upon him judgement and knowledge. And thus do We reward the doers of good.
tl: Wa-lammaa balagha ashuddahoo wastawaaa aatai-naahu hukmanw Wa-'ilmaa; Wa-kazaalika najzil muhsineen

text: And he entered the city at a time of inattention by its people and found therein two men fighting: one from his faction and one from among his enemy. And the one from his faction called for help to him against the one from his enemy, so Moses struck him and [unintentionally] killed him. [Moses] said, \"This is from the work of Satan. Indeed, he is a manifest, misleading enemy.\"
tl: Wa-dakhalal madeenata 'alaa heene ghaflatim min ahlihaa fawajada feeha raju-laini yaqtatilaani haazaa min shee'atihee Wa-haaza min 'aduwwihee fastaghaasahul lazee min shee'atihee 'alal lazee min 'aduwwihee fawakazahoo Moosaa faqadaa 'alaihi qaala haaza min 'amalish Shaitaani innahoo 'aduwwum mmudillum mubeen

text: He said, \"My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, so forgive me,\" and He forgave him. Indeed, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
tl: Qaala Rabbi innee zalamtu nafsee faghfir lee faghafara lah; innahoo Huwal Ghafoorur Raheem

text: He said, \"My Lord, for the favor You bestowed upon me, I will never be an assistant to the criminals.\"
tl: Qaala Rabbi bimaaa an'amta 'alaiya falan akoona zaheeral lilmujrimeen

text: And he became inside the city fearful and anticipating [exposure], when suddenly the one who sought his help the previous day cried out to him [once again]. Moses said to him, \"Indeed, you are an evident, [persistent] deviator.\"
tl: Fa-asbaha fil madeenati khaaa'ifaien yataraqqabu fa-izal lazis tansarahoo bil-amsi yastasrikhuh; qaala lahoo moosaaa innaka laghawiyyum mubeen

text: And when he wanted to strike the one who was an enemy to both of them, he said, \"O Moses, do you intend to kill me as you killed someone yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land and do not want to be of the amenders.\"
tl: Falammaaa an araada aien yabtisha billazee huwa 'aduwwul lahumaa qaala yaa-Moosaaa atureedu an taqtulanee kamaa qatalta nafsam bil-amsi in tureedu illaaa an takoona jabbaaram fil ardi Wa-maa tureedu an takoona minal musliheen

text: And a man came from the farthest end of the city, running. He said, \"O Moses, indeed the eminent ones are conferring over you [intending] to kill you, so leave [the city]; indeed, I am to you of the sincere advisors.\"
tl: Wa-jaaa'a rajulum min aqsal madeenati yas'aa qaala yaa-Moosaaa innal mala-a yaa-tamiroona bika liyaqtulooka fakhruj innee laka minan naasiheen

text: So he left it, fearful and anticipating [apprehension]. He said, \"My Lord, save me from the wrongdoing people.\"
tl: Fakharaja minhaa khaaa 'ifany-yataraqqab; qaala Rabbi najjinee minal qawmiz zaalimeen

text: And when he directed himself toward Madyan, he said, \"Perhaps my Lord will guide me to the sound way.\"
tl: Wa-lammaa tawajjaha tilqaaa'a Madyana qaala 'asaa Rabbeee aien yahdiyanee Sawaaa'as Sabeel

text: And when he came to the water [i.e., well] of Madyan, he found there a crowd of people watering [their flocks], and he found aside from them two women holding back [their flocks]. He said, \"What is your circumstance?\" They said, \"We do not water until the shepherds dispatch [their flocks]; and our father is an old man.\"
tl: Wa-lammaa warada maaa'a Madyana wajada 'alaihi ummatam minannaasi yasqoona Wa-wajada min doonihimum ra ataini tazoodaani qaala maa khatubkumaa qaalataa laa nasqee hataa yusdirar ri'aaa'u Wa-aboonaa shaikhun kabeer

text: So he watered [their flocks] for them; then he went back to the shade and said, \"My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need.\"
tl: Fasaqaa lahumaa summa tawallaaa ilaz zilli faqaala Rabbi innee limaaa anzalta ilaiya min khairin faqeer

text: Then one of the two women came to him walking with shyness. She said, \"Indeed, my father invites you that he may reward you for having watered for us.\" So when he came to him and related to him the story, he said, \"Fear not. You have escaped from the wrongdoing people.\"
tl: Fajaaa'at-hu ihdaahumaa tamshee 'alas tihyaaa'in qaalat inna abee yad'ooka li-yajziyaka ajra maa saqaita lanaa; falammaa jaaa'ahoo Wa-qassa 'alaihil qasasa qaala laa takhaf najawta minal qawmiz zaalimeen

text: One of the women said, \"O my father, hire him. Indeed, the best one you can hire is the strong and the trustworthy.\"
tl: Qaalat ihdaahumaa yaaa abatis taajirhu inna khaira manistaajartal qawiyyul ameen

text: He said, \"Indeed, I wish to wed you one of these, my two daughters, on [the condition] that you serve me for eight years; but if you complete ten, it will be [as a favor] from you. And I do not wish to put you in difficulty. You will find me, if Allāh wills, from among the righteous.\"
tl: Qaala innee ureedu an unkihaka ihdab nataiya haataini 'alaaa an taajuranee samaaniya hijaj; fa-in atmamta 'ashran famin 'indika Wa-maaa ureedu an ashuqqa 'alaik; satajiduneee in shaaa'al laahu minas saaliheen

text: [Moses] said, \"That is [established] between me and you. Whichever of the two terms I complete - there is no injustice to me, and Allāh, over what we say, is Witness.\"
tl: Qaala zaalika bainee Wa-bainaka aiyamal ajalaini qadaitu falaa 'udwaana 'alaiya wallaahu 'alaa ma naqoolu Wakeel

text: And when Moses had completed the term and was traveling with his family, he perceived from the direction of the mount a fire. He said to his family, \"Stay here; indeed, I have perceived a fire. Perhaps I will bring you from there [some] information or burning wood from the fire that you may warm yourselves.\"
tl: Falammmaa qadaa Moosal ajala Wa-saara bi-ahliheee aanasa min jaanibit Toori naaran qaala li-ahlihim kusooo inneee aanastu naaral la-'alleee aateekum minhaa bikhabarin aw jazwatim minan naari la-'allakum tastaloon

text: But when he came to it, he was called from the right side of the valley in a blessed spot - from the tree, \"O Moses, indeed I am Allāh, Lord of the worlds.\"
tl: Falammaaa ataahaa noodiya min shaati'il waadil aimani fil buq'atil muubaarakati minash shajarati aien yaa-Moosaaa inneee Anal laahu Rabbul 'aalameen

text: And [he was told], \"Throw down your staff.\" But when he saw it writhing as if it was a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allāh said], \"O Moses, approach and fear not. Indeed, you are of the secure.
tl: Wa-an alqi 'asaaka falam-maa ra-aahaa tahtazzu ka-annnahaa jaaannunw wallaa mudbiranw Wa-lam yu'aqqib; yaa-Moosaa aqbil Wa-laa takhaf innaka minal aamineen

text: Insert your hand into the opening of your garment; it will come out white, without disease. And draw in your arm close to you [as prevention] from fear, for those are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his establishment. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient.\"
tl: Usluk yadaka fee jaibika takhruj baidaaa'a min ghairi sooo'inw wadmum ilaika janaahaka minar rahbi fazaanika burhaanaani mir Rabbika ilaa Fir'awna Wa-mala'ih; innahum kaanoo qawman faasiqeen

text: He said, \"My Lord, indeed I killed from among them someone, and I fear they will kill me.
tl: Qaala Rabbi innee qataltu minhum nafsan fa-akhaafu aien yaqtuloon

text: And my brother Aaron is more fluent than me in tongue, so send him with me as support, verifying me. Indeed, I fear that they will deny me.\"
tl: Wa-akhee Haaroonu huwa afsahu minnee lisaanan fa-arsilhu ma'iya rid-aien yusaddiquneee innee akhaafu aien yukazziboon

text: [Allāh] said, \"We will strengthen your arm through your brother and grant you both supremacy so they will not reach you. [It will be] through Our signs; you and those who follow you will be the predominant.\"
tl: Qaala sanashuddu 'adudaka bi-akheeka Wa-naj'alu lakumaa sultaanan falaa yasiloona ilaikumaa; bi-Aayaatinaa antumaa Wa-manit taba'akumal ghaaliboon

text: But when Moses came to them with Our signs as clear evidences, they said, \"This is not except invented magic, and we have not heard of this [religion] among our forefathers.\"
tl: Falammaa jaaa'ahum Moosaa bi-Aayaatinaa baiyinaatin qaaloo maa haazaaa illaa sihrum muftaranw Wa-maa sami'naa bihaazaa feee aabaaa'inal awwaleen

text: And Moses said, \"My Lord is more knowing [than we or you] of who has come with guidance from Him and to whom will be succession in the home. Indeed, wrongdoers do not succeed.\"
tl: Wa-qaala Moosaa Rabbeee a'alamu biman jaaa'a bilhudaa min 'indihee Wa-man takoonu lahoo 'aaqibatud daari innahoo laa yuflihuz zaalimoon

text: And Pharaoh said, \"O eminent ones, I have not known you to have a god other than me. Then ignite for me, O Hāmān, [a fire] upon the clay and make for me a tower that I may look at the God of Moses. And indeed, I do think he is among the liars.\"
tl: Wa-qaala Fir'awnu yaaa-aiyyuhal mala-u maa 'alimtu lakum min ilaahin ghairee fa-awqid lee yaa-Haamaanu 'alatteeni faj'al lee sarhal la'alleee attali'u ilaaa ilaahi Moosaa Wa-innee la azunnuhoo minal kaazibeen

text: And he was arrogant, he and his soldiers, in the land, without right, and they thought that they would not be returned to Us.
tl: Wastakbara huwa Wa-junooduhoo fil ardi bighairil haqqi Wa-zannooo annahum ilainaa laa yurja'oon

text: So We took him and his soldiers and threw them into the sea. So see how was the end of the wrongdoers.
tl: Fa-akhaznaahu Wa-junoo-dahoo fanabaznaahum fil yammi fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatuz zaalimeen

text: And We made them leaders inviting to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped.
tl: Wa-ja'alnaahum a'immataien yad'oona ilan Naari Wa-Yawmal Qiyaamati laa yunsaroon

text: And We caused to overtake them in this world a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be of the despised.
tl: Wa-atba'naahum fee haazihid dunyaa la'natanw Wa-Yawmal Qiyaamati hum minal maqbooheen

text: And We gave Moses the Scripture, after We had destroyed the former generations, as enlightenment for the people and guidance and mercy that they might be reminded.
tl: Wa-laqad aatainaa Moosal Kitaaba mim ba'di maaa ahlaknal quroonal oolaa basaaa'ira linnaasi Wa-hudanw Wa-rahmatal la'allahum yata-zakkkaroon

text: And you, [O Muḥammad], were not on the western side [of the mount] when We revealed to Moses the command, and you were not among the witnesses [to that].
tl: Wa-maa kunta bijaanibil gharbiyyi iz qadainaaa ilaa Moosal amra Wa-maa kunta minash shaahideen

text: But We produced [many] generations [after Moses], and prolonged was their duration. And you were not a resident among the people of Madyan, reciting to them Our verses, but We were senders [of this message].
tl: Wa-laakinnaa anshaanaa quroonan fatataawala 'alaihimul 'umur; Wa-maa kunta saawiyan feee ahli Madyana tatloo 'alaihim Aayaatinaa Wa-laakinnaa kunnaa mursileen

text: And you were not at the side of the mount when We called [Moses] but [were sent] as a mercy from your Lord to warn a people to whom no warner had come before you that they might be reminded.
tl: Wa-maa kunta bijaanibit Toori iz naadainaa Wa-laakir rahmatam mir Rabbika litunzira qawmam maaa ataahum min nazeerim min qablika la'allahum yatazakkaroon

text: And if not that a disaster should strike them for what their hands put forth [of sins] and they would say, \"Our Lord, why did You not send us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses and been among the believers?\"...
tl: Wa-law-laaa an tuseebahum museebatum bimaa qaddamat aideehim fa-yaqooloo Rabbanaa law-laaa arsalta ilainaa Rasoolan fanattabi'a Aayaatika Wa-nakoona minal mu'mineen

text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, \"Why was he not given like that which was given to Moses?\" Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Moses before? They said, \"[They are but] two works of magic supporting each other, and indeed we are, in both, disbelievers.\"
tl: Falammaa jaaa'ahumul haqqu min 'indinaa qaaloo law-laa ootiya misla maaa ootiyaa Moosaa; awalam yakfuroo bimaaa ootiya Moosaa min qablu qaaloo sihraani tazaaharaa Wa-qaalooo innaa bikullin kaafiroon

text: Say, \"Then bring a scripture from Allāh which is more guiding than either of them that I may follow it, if you should be truthful.\"
tl: Qul faatoo bi-Kitaabim min 'indil laahi huwa ahdaa minhu-maaa attabi'hu in kuntum saadiqeen

text: But if they do not respond to you - then know that they only follow their [own] desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from Allāh? Indeed, Allāh does not guide the wrongdoing people.
tl: Fa-il lam yastajeeboo laka fa'lam annamaa yattabi'oona ahwaaa'ahum; Wa-man adallu mimmanit taba'a hawaahu bighari hudam minal laah; innal laaha laa yahdil qawmaz zaalimeen

text: And We have [repeatedly] conveyed to them the word [i.e., the Qur’ān] that they might be reminded.
tl: Wa-laqad wassalnaa lahumul qawla la'allahum yatazakkaroon

text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it - they are believers in it.
tl: Allazeena aatainaahu-mul Kitaaba min qablihee hum bihee yu'minoon

text: And when it is recited to them, they say, \"We have believed in it; indeed, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were, [even] before it, Muslims [i.e., submitting to Allāh].\"
tl: Wa-izaa yutlaa 'alaihim qaaloo aamannaa biheee innahul haqqu mir rabbinaaa innaa kunnaa min qablihee muslimeen

text: Those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured and [because] they avert evil through good, and from what We have provided them they spend.
tl: Ulaaa'ika yu'tawna ajrahum marratayni bimaa sabaroo Wa-yadra'oona bil-hasanatis saiyyi'ata Wa-mimmmaa razaq-naahum yunfiqoon

text: And when they hear ill speech, they turn away from it and say, \"For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. Peace will be upon you; we seek not the ignorant.\"
tl: Wa-izaa sami'ul laghwa a'radoo 'anhu Wa-qaaloo lanaaa a'maalunaa Wa-lakum a'maalukum salaamun 'alaikum laa nabtaghil jaahileen

text: Indeed, [O Muḥammad], you do not guide whom you like, but Allāh guides whom He wills. And He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
tl: Innaka laa tahdee man ahbata Wa-laakinna laaha yahdee maien yashaaa'; Wa-Huwaa 'lamu bilmuhtadeen

text: And they [i.e., the Quraysh] say, \"If we were to follow the guidance with you, we would be swept from our land.\" Have We not established for them a safe sanctuary to which are brought the fruits of all things as provision from Us? But most of them do not know.
tl: Wa-qaalooo in nattabi'il hudaa ma'aka nutakhattaf min ardinaa; awalam numakkkil lahum haraman aaminany yujbaaa ilaihi samaraatu kulli shai'ir rizqam mil ladunnaa Wa-laakinna aksarahum laa ya'lamoon

text: And how many a city have We destroyed that was insolent in its [way of] living, and those are their dwellings which have not been inhabited after them except briefly. And it is We who were the inheritors.
tl: Wa-kam ahlaknaa min qaryatim batirat ma'eeshatahaa fatilka masaakinuhum lam tuskam mim ba'dihim illaa qaleelaa; Wa-kunnaa Nahnul waariseen

text: And never would your Lord have destroyed the cities until He had sent to their mother [i.e., principal city] a messenger reciting to them Our verses. And We would not destroy the cities except while their people were wrongdoers.
tl: Wa-maa kaana Rabbuka muhlikal quraa hattaa yab'asa feee ummihaa Rasoolany yatloo 'alaihim aayaatina; Wa-maa kunnaa muhlikil quraaa illaa Wa-ahluhaa zaalimoon

text: And whatever thing you [people] have been given - it is [only for] the enjoyment of worldly life and its adornment. And what is with Allāh is better and more lasting; so will you not use reason?
tl: Wa-maaa ooteetum min shai'in famataa'ul hayaatid dunyaa Wa-zeenatuhaa; Wa-maa 'indal laahi khairunw Wa-abqaa; afalaa ta'qiloon

text: Then is he whom We have promised a good promise which he will meet [i.e., obtain] like he for whom We provided enjoyment of worldly life [but] then he is, on the Day of Resurrection, among those presented [for punishment in Hell]?
tl: Afamanw wa'adnaahu wa'dan hasanan fahuwa laaqeehi kamam matta'naahu mataa'al hayaatid dunyaa summa huwa Yawmal Qiyaamati minal muhdareen

text: And [warn of] the Day He will call them and say, \"Where are My 'partners' which you used to claim?\"
tl: Wa-Yawma yunaadeehim fa-yaqoolu aina shurakaaa-'iyal lazeena kuntum taz'umoon

text: Those upon whom the word will have come into effect will say, \"Our Lord, these are the ones we led to error. We led them to error just as we were in error. We declare our disassociation [from them] to You. They did not used to worship [i.e., obey] us.\"
tl: Qaalal lazeena haqqa 'alaihimul qawlu Rabbanaa haaa'ulaaa'il lazeena aghwainaa aghwainaahu kamaa ghawainaa tabarraanaaa ilaika maa kaanoo iyyaanaa ya'budoon

text: And it will be said, \"Invoke your 'partners,'\" and they will invoke them; but they will not respond to them, and they will see the punishment. If only they had followed guidance!
tl: Wa-qeelad 'oo shurakaaa'akum fada'awhum falam yastajeeboo lahum Wa-ra-awul 'azaab; law annahum kaanoo yahtadoon

text: And [mention] the Day He will call them and say, \"What did you answer the messengers?\"
tl: Wa-Yawma yunaadeehim fa-yaqoolu maazaaa ajabtumul mursaleen

text: But the information will be unapparent to them that Day, so they will not [be able to] ask one another.
tl: Fa'amiyat 'alaihimul ambaaa'u Yawma'izin fahum laa yatasaaa'aloon

text: But as for one who had repented, believed, and done righteousness, it is expected [i.e., promised by Allāh] that he will be among the successful.
tl: Fa-ammaa man taaba Wa-aamana Wa-'amila saalihan fa'asaaa ai yakoona minal mufliheen

text: And your Lord creates what He wills and chooses; not for them was the choice. Exalted is Allāh and high above what they associate with Him.
tl: Wa-Rabbuka yakhuluqu maa yashaaa'u Wa-yakhtaar; maa kaana lahumul khiyarah; Subhannal laahi Wa-Ta'aalaa 'ammmaa yushrikoon

text: And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.
tl: Wa-Rabbuka ya'lamu maa tukinnu sudooruhum Wa-maa yu'linoon

text: And He is Allāh; there is no deity except Him. To Him is [due all] praise in the first [life] and the Hereafter. And His is the [final] decision, and to Him you will be returned.
tl: Wa-Huwal laahu laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa lahul hamdu fil oolaa wal-Aakhirati Wa-lahul hukmu Wa-ilaihi turja'oon

text: Say, \"Have you considered: if Allāh should make for you the night continuous until the Day of Resurrection, what deity other than Allāh could bring you light? Then will you not hear?\"
tl: Qul ara'aitum in ja'alal laahu 'alaikumul laila sarmadan ilaa Yawmil Qiyaamati man ilaahun ghairul laahi yaa-teekum bidiyaaa'in afalaa tasma'oon

text: Say, \"Have you considered: if Allāh should make for you the day continuous until the Day of Resurrection, what deity other than Allāh could bring you a night in which you may rest? Then will you not see?\"
tl: Qul ara'aitum in ja'alal laahu 'alaikumun nahaara sarmadan ilaa Yawmil Qiyaamati man ilaahun ghairul laahi yaateekum bilailin taskunoona feehi afalaa tubsiroon

text: And out of His mercy He made for you the night and the day that you may rest therein and [by day] seek from His bounty and [that] perhaps you will be grateful.
tl: Wa-mir rahmatihee ja'ala lakumul laila wannahaara litaskunoo feehi Wa-litabtaghoo min fadlihee Wa-la'allakum tashkuroon

text: And [warn of] the Day He will call them and say, \"Where are My 'partners' which you used to claim?\"
tl: Wa-Yawma yunaadeehim fa-yaqoolu aina shurakaaa'iyal lazeena kuntum tazz'umoon

text: And We will extract from every nation a witness and say, \"Produce your proof,\" and they will know that the truth belongs to Allāh, and lost from them is that which they used to invent.
tl: Wa-naza'naa min kulli ummatin shaheedan faqulnaa haatoo burhaanakum fa'alimooo annal haqqa lillaahi Wa-dalla 'anhum maa kaanoo yaftaroon

text: Indeed, Qārūn was from the people of Moses, but he tyrannized them. And We gave him of treasures whose keys would burden a band of strong men; thereupon his people said to him, \"Do not exult. Indeed, Allāh does not like the exultant.
tl: Inna Qaaroona kaana min qawmi Moosaa fabaghaaa 'alaihim Wa-aatainaahu minal kunoozi maaa inna mafaati-hahoo latanooo'u bil'usbati ulil quwwati iz qaala lahoo qawmuhoo laa tafrah innal laahaa laa yuhibbul fariheen

text: But seek, through that which Allāh has given you, the home of the Hereafter; and [yet], do not forget your share of the world. And do good as Allāh has done good to you. And desire not corruption in the land. Indeed, Allāh does not like corrupters.\"
tl: Wabtaghi feemaaa aataakal laahud Daaral Aakhirata Wa-laa tansa naseebaka minad dunyaa Wa-ahsin kamaaa ahsanal laahu ilaika Wa-laa tabghil fasaada fil ardi innal laaha laa yuhibbul mufsideen

text: He said, \"I was only given it because of knowledge I have.\" Did he not know that Allāh had destroyed before him of generations those who were greater than him in power and greater in accumulation [of wealth]? But the criminals, about their sins, will not be asked.
tl: Qaala innamaaa ootee-tuhoo 'alaa 'ilmin 'indeee; awalam ya'lam annal laaha qad ahlaka min qablihee minal qurooni man huwa ashaddu minhu quwwatanw Wa-aksaru jam'aa; Wa-laa yus'alu 'an zunoobihimul mujrimoon

text: So he came out before his people in his adornment. Those who desired the worldly life said, \"Oh, would that we had like what was given to Qārūn. Indeed, he is one of great fortune.\"
tl: Fakharaja 'alaa qawmihee fee zeenatih; qaalal lazeena yureedoonal hayaatad dunyaa yaalaita lanaa misla maaa ootiya Qaaroonu innahoo lazoo hazzin 'azeem

text: But those who had been given knowledge said, \"Woe to you! The reward of Allāh is better for he who believes and does righteousness. And none are granted it except the patient.\"
tl: Wa-qaalal lazeena ootul 'ilma wailakum sawaabul laahi khairul liman aamana Wa-'amila saalihaa; Wa-laa yulaq-qaahaaa illas saabiroon

text: And We caused the earth to swallow him and his home. And there was for him no company to aid him other than Allāh, nor was he of those who [could] defend themselves.
tl: Fakhasafnaa bihee Wa-bidaarihil arda famaa kaana laho min fi'atien yansuroo-nahoo min doonil laahi Wa-maa kaana minal muntasireen

text: And those who had wished for his position the previous day began to say, \"Oh, how Allāh extends provision to whom He wills of His servants and restricts it! If not that Allāh had conferred favor on us, He would have caused it to swallow us. Oh, how the disbelievers do not succeed!\"
tl: Wa-asbahal lazeena tamannaw makaanahoo bil-amsi yaqooloona waika-annal laaha yabsutur rizqa limaien ya-shaaa'u min 'ibaadihee Wa-yaqdiru law-laaa am mannal laahu 'alainaa lakhasafa binaa waika-annahoo laa yuflihul kaafiroon

text: That home of the Hereafter We assign to those who do not desire exaltedness upon the earth or corruption. And the [best] outcome is for the righteous.
tl: Tilkad Daarul Aakhiratu naj'aluhaa lillazeena laa yureedoona 'uluwwan fil ardi Wa-laa fasaadaa; wal-'aaqibatu lilmuttaqeen

text: Whoever comes [on the Day of Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it; and whoever comes with an evil deed - then those who did evil deeds will not be recompensed except [as much as] what they used to do.
tl: Man jaaa'a bilhasanati falahoo khairum minhaa Wa-man jaaa'a bissaiyi'ati falaa yujzal lazeena 'amilus saiyiaati illaa maa kaanoo ya'maloon

text: Indeed, [O Muḥammad], He who imposed upon you the Qur’ān will take you back to a place of return. Say, \"My Lord is most knowing of who brings guidance and who is in clear error.\"
tl: Innal azee farada 'alaikal Qur-aana laraaadduka ilaa ma'aad; qur Rabbeee a'lamu man jjaaa'a bil-hudaa Wa-man huwa fee dalaalim mubeen

text: And you were not expecting that the Book would be conveyed to you, but [it is] a mercy from your Lord. So do not be an assistant to the disbelievers.
tl: Wa-maa kunta tarjooo aien yulqaaa ilaikal Kitaabu illaa rahmatam mir Rabbika falaa takoonanna zaheeral lilkaafireen

text: And never let them avert you from the verses of Allāh after they have been revealed to you. And invite [people] to your Lord. And never be of those who associate others with Allāh.
tl: Wa-laa yasuddunnaka 'an Aayaatil laahi ba'da iz unzilat ilaika wad'u ilaa Rabbika Wa-laa takonanna minal mushrikeen

text: And do not invoke with Allāh another deity. There is no deity except Him. Everything will be destroyed except His Face. His is the judgement, and to Him you will be returned.
tl: Wa-laa tad'u ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhar; laaa ilaaha illaa Hoo; kullu shai'in haalikun illaa Wajhah; lahul hukkmu Wa-ilaihi turja'oon
